title: GoWarm Prospect Context Payload
description: The semantic, CRM-agnostic prospect payload returned by GoWarm's /api/skill-context/prospects/:id endpoint. Designed to support all prospecting-module skills (outreach-personalization, reply-triage, handoff-brief, etc.) via a core shape plus optional slices. Fields use canonical semantic names; field_map resolution from Salesforce/HubSpot/Dynamics runs in the GoWarm backend before the skill ever sees the data.

seniority_level.description: Normalized seniority band. Drives buyer-committee-map and disqualify-check.

tenure_in_role_months.description: How long the prospect has held their current title. New-in-role (<6 months) is a signal.

seat_count_under.description: Estimated headcount reporting into this prospect. Drives deal-size inference.

headline.description: The prospect's LinkedIn headline, verbatim. Quotable; not paraphrasable into claims.

about.description: The prospect's LinkedIn 'About' section, verbatim. Quotable; not paraphrasable into claims.

description.description: Prospect's own words from LinkedIn, if available. Quotable.

education.description: Education history. Use sparingly — fake-commonality failure mode risk is highest here.

skills_listed.description: LinkedIn self-declared skills. Noisy — outreach-personalization should generally ignore. Present for other skills that might use them.

growth_stage.description: Company stage. Shapes hook selection (early-stage prospects respond differently than public-co prospects).

tech_stack.description: Known technology stack. Each entry should indicate source.

fit_score.description: ICP fit score computed by the GoWarm rules engine. Not computed by the skill.

matched_criteria.description: ICP criteria this prospect matches. Skills can reference these as validated fit reasons.

missed_criteria.description: ICP criteria this prospect does NOT match. Inform disqualify-check and confidence_notes.

persona_match.description: Named persona matched, e.g. 'vp_revops_growth_saas'. Null if no persona match.

signals.description: Evidence the skill can cite. Every signal carries a source and timestamp so skills reference provenance instead of generating from priors.

account_events.description: Things that happened to the company — funding, leadership changes, layoffs, product launches, earnings, hiring surges. Usable as trigger events.

description.description: One-line summary, e.g. 'Series C raised ($55M, led by Sequoia)'.

linkedin_activity.description: Prospect-level LinkedIn activity. Posts and substantive comments are usable for direct citation. Reactions are present but guarded — see outreach-principles.md.

posts.description: Posts authored by the prospect. Strongest personalization signal — quotable verbatim.

topic_tags.description: Optional auto-extracted topics, e.g. ['forecasting', 'revops']. Do not treat as authoritative.

comments.description: Comments the prospect wrote on other people's posts. Usable only with parent_post_summary for context.

parent_post_summary.description: Brief description of what the comment was replying to. Without this, comment is unusable.

reactions.description: Posts the prospect reacted to (liked, celebrated, etc.). LAST-RESORT SIGNAL — outreach-personalization skill should never cite these in the email body. See outreach-principles.md.

engagement_history.description: Touches between the rep and prospect. Usually empty or sparse for new prospects. Populated for re-engagement and reply-triage skills.

sequence_state.description: Present only when the prospect is mid-sequence. Informs sequence-design and re-engagement skills.

reply_payload.description: Present only when a skill is triggered by an inbound reply (reply-triage, objection-handler). Absent for first-touch skills like outreach-personalization.

org_context.description: The rep's own company, product, and positioning. In Phase 1 passed per-request from the caller. In production, fetched from tenant config.

one_liner.description: One-sentence description of what the product does.

value_props.description: Short, claim-based value propositions. The skill may draw on these but must not fabricate new ones.

target_personas.description: Named personas the product is built for. Helps the skill judge persona fit.

case_study_summaries.description: Short summaries of real case studies, with source. Skill must only reference these verbatim — never fabricate stats.

customer.description: Can be anonymized, e.g. 'a Series C SaaS company'.

stat.description: Verifiable stat, if any. Use verbatim or not at all.

competitors.description: Known competitors. Skills may reference if prospect's tech_stack includes one.
